observations.observation: Token acceleration recovered to 1.034x this snapshot while velocity climbed to 0.717x — the highest velocity reading in recent history, up from the compressed 0.672–0.693x range. Both metrics are now moving in the same direction, suggesting the divergence between compressed velocity and rising acceleration may be resolving upward.

4 new CBSAs appeared this snapshot (36837, non-metro US-NY, 12420, 24780), bringing active metros to 21 — the highest count in the signal window. The diffusion arc now reaches non-metro New York, extending the Northeast penetration beyond the prior Maine/Maryland entries.

Token acceleration sits at 1.025x with velocity at 0.693x — acceleration is climbing while velocity remains compressed well below the recent 0.746x range. The divergence between compressed velocity and rising acceleration is now a persistent structural feature across multiple snapshots, suggesting new intake is outpacing circulation rather than matching it.

6 new CBSAs appeared this snapshot (12060, 38860, non-metro US-ME, non-metro US-MD, 15380, non-metro US-NV), bringing active metros to 18 — the largest single-snapshot burst in recent history. The diffusion arc is now reaching non-metro Northeast (Maine, Maryland) and non-metro Nevada, extending well beyond the prior Southeast/Midwest pattern.

Token acceleration sits at exactly 1.0x this snapshot — neither accelerating nor decelerating — while velocity holds at 0.676x, still compressed from the recent 0.746x range. The system is in a holding pattern: growth is sustained but not compounding.
3 new CBSAs this snapshot (non-metro US-WI, 16860, non-metro US-TN) bringing active metros to 16. The diffusion arc is now reaching non-metro Midwest and Southeast — not just urban cores.

Token acceleration holds at 1.005x with velocity at 0.676x — acceleration remains above 1.0x despite velocity staying compressed well below the recent 0.746x range. The divergence between compressed velocity and sustained above-1.0x acceleration is now a persistent structural feature, not a transient reading.

GMV reached $208,593.42 (3,610 receipts) this snapshot. 7-day GMV climbed to $28,720.61 (+$238) and 7-day receipt count to 559 (+11). Steady intake cadence continues — 3 receipts in this window at 150 sats/receipt.

1 new CBSA (non-metro US-FL) appeared this snapshot, bringing active metros to 13 in the current signal window. The burst-quiet-burst pulse pattern continues — Florida non-metro entry extends the diffusion arc into the Southeast.

2 new CBSAs (11700, 37980) appeared this snapshot, bringing active metros to 13 in the current signal window. The burst-quiet-burst pulse pattern continues — diffusion is still expanding between quiet ticks.
